rationale.automation-bias: Professionals over-trusted fluent outputs and discovered subtle flaws only later.
rationale.ai-code-insecurity: Developers reported AI-generated insecure patterns and hidden security vulnerabilities.
rationale.quality-debt: Participants encountered flawed logic, inappropriate suggestions, and substantial rewriting.
rationale.rules-based-checks: Participants tested AI outputs; authors advocate static analysis validation workflows.
rationale.survey-input: Interviews provide substantive findings on adoption, trust, security, and training priorities.
rationale.assistive: Copilot and ChatGPT suggestions remain embedded in human-led development workflows.
rationale.steering: Prompt engineering and iterative prompting are substantive documented developer practices.
rationale.risk-security: Security vulnerabilities and validation practices receive focal empirical analysis.
rationale.risk-quality: The study reports flawed logic, poor fit, and rewriting burdens.
rationale.risk-overreliance: False confidence, dependency, deskilling, and reduced critical engagement are central.
rationale.method-self-report: Fifteen professionals described lived experiences through semi-structured interviews.
